title: Police Officer

rationales.taskAutomation: Patrol, use-of-force decisions, and in-person community contact dominate the job; only incident report drafting and records lookups are reliably automatable today.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI report-writing assistants (e.g. body-cam-to-narrative tools) and license-plate/analytics platforms are real and improving fast, but remain add-ons rather than the core toolchain.
rationales.adoption: Most departments are still piloting or budget-constrained; only well-funded metro forces have moved report-writing and analytics tools into daily use.
rationales.agenticExposure: The core of the job is physical presence and split-second judgment in the field, which autonomous software cannot reach.

tasksAtRisk: Drafting routine incident and arrest reports
Transcribing body-camera footage into narrative form
tasksGrowing: Interpreting AI-flagged leads and evidence with legal judgment
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 33-3051, p25-p75 rounded
insight: Patrol work itself sits outside AI's reach, but the paperwork half of the job is being rewritten: departments that adopted AI report-drafting tools report officers spending far less time behind a keyboard. Officers who master those tools free up the in-person hours that actually define the job.
